snippets.title: Discrete expectation and variance by hand
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

# X ~ fair die: values 1..6, equal PMF
x = np.arange(1, 7)
p = np.ones(6) / 6

mean = np.sum(x * p)
variance = np.sum((x - mean) ** 2 * p)
# equivalent: np.sum(x**2 * p) - mean**2

print(f"E[X] = {mean}")
print(f"Var(X) = {variance:.4f}, std = {np.sqrt(variance):.4f}")
snippets.explanation: Discrete expectation is a weighted sum Σ x p(x). Variance uses the same weights on squared deviations, or E[X²] − (E[X])².

snippets.title: SciPy distribution moments
snippets.code:
from scipy import stats

# X ~ Binomial(n=10, p=0.3)
X = stats.binom(n=10, p=0.3)

print(X.mean())      # E[X] = n*p = 3.0
print(X.var())       # Var(X) = n*p*(1-p) = 2.1
print(X.stats(moments='mv'))  # (mean, variance)
snippets.explanation: Named distributions expose `.mean()` and `.var()` implementing closed-form E[X] and Var(X) from their PMF/PDF definitions.

snippets.title: Sample mean, variance and NumPy
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

latency_ms = np.array([95, 110, 130, 88, 250, 105, 115])

sample_mean = np.mean(latency_ms)
sample_var = np.var(latency_ms, ddof=1)  # unbiased: divide by n-1

print(f"x̄ = {sample_mean:.1f} ms")
print(f"s² = {sample_var:.1f}, s = {np.sqrt(sample_var):.1f} ms")
snippets.explanation: `np.mean` estimates E[X]; `np.var(..., ddof=1)` matches the unbiased sample variance with divisor n−1.

snippets.title: Linearity of expectation with indicators
snippets.code:
import numpy as np

# Simulate 100k users: 40% see variant A (revenue 3), 60% see B (revenue 4)
np.random.seed(0)
assign_a = np.random.rand(100_000) < 0.4
revenue = np.where(assign_a, 3.0, 4.0)

print(revenue.mean())  # ≈ 3.6 = 0.4*3 + 0.6*4
snippets.explanation: The sample mean of per-user revenue converges to E[revenue] by linearity: a weighted sum of arm expectations.
